name: Payan, Beth
resolved_employer: Travis County Attorney's Office (Assistant County Attorney); formerly 25+ yrs Travis County District Attorney's Office felony prosecutor
evidence: Beth Fagin Payan, Austin attorney listed on the Travis County Attorney's Office (TCAO) attorney roster, matching the filing's occupation 'Attorney' and employer 'Travis County'. Career: Assistant City Attorney for City of Austin, then 25+ years as a Travis County DA felony prosecutor (supervisor of Child Abuse/Juvenile, Sexual Assault, Diversion and Trial Court divisions), a stint in criminal defense at Jason S. English Law PLLC, and now TCAO. UT Austin 1989, St. Mary's Law 1992. She filed as a Democratic candidate for Justice, 3rd Court of Appeals Place 4 in the 2026 cycle. NOT the Elizabeth Christian Public Relations firm - the ECPR staff/leadership pages (Elizabeth Christian, Kristin Marcum) return no 'Beth Payan', and the Travis County attorney identification is corroborated by employer, occupation and Austin residence.

affiliations.snippet: Her campaign bio states she 'is active with Mother Attorneys Mentoring Association and currently on the board of the Capital Area Democratic Women.'
'From volunteering on Governor Ann Richards' successful 1990 campaign to serving as Precinct Chair with the Travis County Democratic Party, Beth is an engaged Democratic activist.'

affiliations.org: Beth Payan for Justice (3rd Court of Appeals, Place 4)
affiliations.role: 2026 Democratic judicial candidate (subsequently withdrew from the race)
affiliations.snippet: Campaign site and Ballotpedia list Beth Payan as a candidate for Justice of the 3rd Court of Appeals, Place 4, campaigning to 'flip the last Republican-held seat on the 3rd Court of Appeals'; she later withdrew.
'Beth is active with Mother Attorneys Mentoring Association.'

name: Franklin, Marie

evidence: Marie Franklin of 15000 Hog Eye Rd, Manor TX 78653 - the same address as the registered business Barry JW Franklin LLC / Franklin Barry (Manor, TX 78653-4905) - is listed on the A Life Celebration by Franklin staff page as 'Mrs. Marie Franklin, Business Office Specialist and Family Support Services.' No public profile beyond the family business was found; her 6 small FEC/ActBlue-style federal donations are consistent with ordinary Democratic small-dollar giving and do not show ideological PAC ties.

name: Manning-Scott, Michelle
resolved_employer: KOOP Radio 91.7 FM (Austin community radio) - volunteer producer and co-editor/host of the syndicated 'Democracy Now!' broadcast; the filing's employer 'Democracy Now!' refers to the KOOP-aired program she edits, not the New York-based national newsroom
evidence: Michelle Manning-Scott moved to Austin in 2018, studied mass communications in Boston and Fort Worth, and has been on KOOP Radio's volunteer production team since early 2019. KOOP's own profile of her states she and Ken McKenzie-Grant 'edit and air the syndicated Democracy Now! show, 2-3pm on Thursdays' - directly matching the filing's occupation 'Editor' and employer 'Democracy Now!' plus her Austin 78757 address. She was associate producer on KOOP's 'Civil Rights and Wrongs' and produces station promos and PSAs. Her 12 federal donations with a fully Democratic lean are consistent with the small-dollar progressive giving pattern described in her profile; no ideological or industry PAC ties surfaced.

name: Ramos, Amos
resolved_employer: Self-reported 'Not Employed' on the filing; a LinkedIn profile for an Amos Ramos located in Royal Oak, MI describes a marketing and sales professional with national and international retail experience, most recently associated with Gulf Oil (University of Miami)
evidence: Donor gave $450 to Misael Ramos from Royal Oak, MI 48067 listing occupation 'Not Employed'. The only public footprint for the uncommon name 'Amos Ramos' in Royal Oak is a LinkedIn profile for a marketing/sales professional in retail located in Royal Oak, MI, shown in search results as associated with Gulf Oil; a public-records aggregator also lists an Amos Ramos at a Royal Oak 48067 address. City + uncommon-name match corroborate identity, but the LinkedIn profile page itself could not be loaded (HTTP 999) so the employment status/currency is unverified and conflicts with the filing's 'Not Employed'. NOTE: no public source found supports any family relationship to candidate Misael Ramos; his published biography describes a Dallas, TX upbringing with no Michigan connection, so no relationship is asserted here. The Gulf Oil association appears to be a sales/marketing role, not an executive, board, or ownership position, so no oil_gas affiliation is recorded.

name: King, David
evidence: Cannot confidently identify this donor. The prominent Austin land-use figure David King -- longtime Austin Neighborhoods Council member, briefly ANC president, Zoning and Platting Commission appointee of CM Ann Kitchen, CodeNEXT opponent, 'retired from the state' -- is a poor fit because he DIED on June 6, 2024 (Austin Monitor), well before this 2026-02-28 contribution; he is therefore affirmatively excluded, not merely unconfirmed. 'David King' is an extremely common name in Austin and no other public record ties a David King in 78704 with occupation 'Retired' to a specific former career. A separate State Bar of Texas attorney 'David Austin King' exists but does not match the self-reported 'Retired' occupation. Per instructions, industry left null at low confidence rather than guessed. (The pipeline's 213 FEC-donation / lean-1.0 signal indicates a prolific small-dollar Democratic donor but does not by itself identify the individual.)

evidence: Joint household donation confirmed in City of Austin campaign finance open data: 'Askew, Robert and Erin', Austin TX 78704, occupation 'Physician', employer 'Austin Surgeons', $900.00 on 2025-12-13 to Steven A. Brown. The employer+occupation match Dr. Robert E. Askew Jr., MD, a general/robotic/laparoscopic surgeon who joined his father's Austin practice in 1991 and practices at Austin Surgeons (3901 Medical Pkwy). Note: the project's FEC match records a lean of 0.0 (Republican-leaning) across 13 federal donations for this household; the specific committees could not be re-verified this session because the FEC OpenFEC API returned HTTP 429 (rate limit) on every attempt, and no PAC ties in any tracked category surfaced via web search. Erin Askew could not be independently identified — several unrelated Erin Askews exist in Austin — so no separate occupation is asserted for her.

affiliations.role: Board member (Robert E. Askew Jr., MD)

name: Jonathan, Scott
resolved_employer: CM Law (Culhane Meadows / CM Law PLLC) -- Partner, Austin office
evidence: The filed name is reversed: this is Jonathan Scott, a Partner at CM Law with Austin as his primary office, matching the filing's employer 'CM Law' and occupation 'Partner' exactly. Full firm bio read: 15 years at the SEC Division of Enforcement (Washington DC and Fort Worth) supervising insider-trading, market-manipulation, accounting-fraud and FCPA investigations; five Enforcement Director awards and a Chairman's Award; then six years as global Chief Compliance Officer at Gerson Lehrman Group; earlier at Fried Frank. JD American University (1995, summa cum laude), BS Boston University. Joined CM Law in 2025. Independently corroborated by an FEC-derived contribution record for a Jonathan Scott in Austin listing employer 'Gerson Lehrman Group', occupation 'Compliance Officer' ($10 to ActBlue, 09/18/2020, zip 78703 -- consistent with an in-Austin move to 78757). Listed memberships are professional/bar bodies only (ASECA, SEC Historical Society, ABA, Austin Bar Association) with no policy-advocacy component, and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration was found under this name.

name: Seufert, Eric
evidence: Employer-fallback classification only. The best-known Eric Seufert is Eric Benjamin Seufert, a media strategist who runs Heracles, a one-person strategy consultancy, plus the Mobile Dev Memo trade publication and Heracles Capital, and is listed as an analyst at ITIF; his self-employed-consultant profile fits the filing's 'Consultant / Self employed', and he holds a BBA in Finance from UT Austin. However his published location is Greater Boston, not Tomball TX 77377, and the only Tomball link is a people-search aggregator listing an Eric Seufert (42) with prior Tomball, TX residence and a relative in Tomball -- aggregator data that is not a reliable identity match. Identity therefore marked low; the industry label reflects only the self-reported occupation/employer, which holds regardless of which Eric Seufert this is. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration and no federal PAC contributions surfaced under this name.

name: Brion, Oaks
resolved_employer: Texas Campaign for the Environment — Executive Director (name is reversed in the filing; correct name is Brion Oaks)
evidence: Name reversal confirmed: City of Austin campaign finance data contains two records dated 2026-03-03, both Round Rock TX 78665, occupation 'Non profit', employer 'Texas Campaign for the Environment', $450 each — one filed as 'Oaks, Brion' (to Richard Heyman) and one filed as 'Brion, Oaks' (to Misael D. Ramos), i.e. the Ramos filer transposed the name. The same 78665 address appears in 2024 records with employer 'Charles Butt Foundation' / 'Equity Office', matching Brion Oaks' documented career path exactly: inaugural City of Austin Chief Equity Officer, then VP of Operations and DEI at the Charles Butt Foundation, now TCE Executive Director. Texas Campaign for the Environment employment is verified on TCE's own staff bio page. No Brion Oaks appears in Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists; no federal PAC contributions surfaced (OpenFEC API was rate-limited, HTTP 429, all session).

affiliations.snippet: TCE bio: Oaks brings 'more than 20 years of executive leadership advancing racial equity, environmental justice, and community-driven policy change' and is 'committed to building on TCE/TCE Fund's grassroots legacy.' TCE is an environmental advocacy organization that publicly campaigns against corporate polluters and testifies before the TCEQ.
Oaks was appointed the City of Austin's first Chief Equity Officer, responsible for creating an equity framework for adoption across all city departments and offices.

affiliations.role: Vice President of Operations and DEI (former)
affiliations.snippet: TCE bio states he 'held the position of Vice President of Operations and DEI for the Charles Butt Foundation'; corroborated by 2024 Austin campaign finance filings listing Charles Butt Foundation as his employer at the same Round Rock address.

name: Gomez, Margaret
evidence: Identity confirmed by donation-history continuity at a single address in City of Austin campaign finance data: 'Gomez, Margaret', Austin TX 78704 / 78704-5950, listed as occupation 'County Commissioner', employer 'Travis County' on contributions in 2024 (to Gary Bledsoe) and 2025-12-03 (to Marc Duchen), then as 'Retired' on the 2026-04-07 $450 contribution to Steven A. Brown — matching her 2026 retirement. This is Margaret Gómez, the longest-serving commissioner in Travis County history, first elected commissioner in 1995 and the first Mexican-American woman in the role; she stepped down in June 2026 after nearly 53 years as a county employee. Zip 78704 is consistent with Precinct 4, which covers nearly all of South Austin east of South Lamar. Not listed in Texas Ethics Commission registered-lobbyist lists for 2021–2026. The project's FEC match shows lean 1.0 (Democratic) across 928 federal donations, consistent with a longtime Democratic officeholder's recurring small-dollar giving; specific committees could not be enumerated this session because the OpenFEC API returned HTTP 429 on every attempt, and no PAC ties in any tracked category surfaced via web search.

affiliations.role: County Commissioner, 1995–2026 (retired); previously Travis County Constable from 1980
affiliations.snippet: Gómez, 81, the longest-serving Travis County commissioner in history, retired after nearly 53 years as a county employee; she became a commissioner in 1995, the first Mexican-American woman in the role, and had unseated a 14-year incumbent constable in 1980.

name: Cobb, Gary
resolved_employer: Travis County District Attorney's Office — Assistant DA / Director, Grand Jury & Intake Division (retired); later of counsel at Herring & Panzer, L.L.P.
evidence: Best match is Gary Allan Cobb, longtime Travis County assistant district attorney (24 years, director of the Grand Jury/Intake Division), UT Law 1986, who ran in the 2008 and 2016 Democratic primaries for Travis County DA and said after losing to Margaret Moore in 2016 that he would not stay at the office. Zip 78749 is corroborated by contemporaneous reporting describing him as vice president of the Southwest Austin Democrats (78749 is southwest Austin) and by a City of Austin filing listing a 512-899 (southwest Austin) home number. 'Retired' occupation and a heavily Democratic 92-donation federal giving history (FEC lean 0.90) are consistent with his documented Democratic-primary political activity. Confidence held at medium because no public record directly ties the 78749 address to this specific Gary Cobb, and there are other Austin-area Gary Cobbs (including one whose obituary appears in 2019). Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile and Martindale/State Bar listings found no lobbying, energy, firearms, defense, or Israel/Palestine-related roles on any side; no TEC lobbyist registration found.

affiliations.snippet: Cobb is described as a past president of the Austin Black Lawyers Association.
Former chair of the board of directors of KAZI, Austin Community Radio.
Cobb is vice president of the Southwest Austin Democrats and a past president of the Austin Black Lawyers Association.

affiliations.role: Assistant District Attorney; director, Grand Jury/Intake Division; two-time Democratic primary candidate for District Attorney (2008, 2016)
affiliations.snippet: Margaret Moore defeated Gary Cobb, a longtime Travis County prosecutor, in the 2016 Democratic primary for Travis County District Attorney.
